content: {"personas": [{"perspective": "Scientific", "name": "Dr. Elena Rossi", "expertise": "Clinical endocrinologist and meta-analyst specializing in metabolic syndrome trials. Author on multiple GRADE-rated systematic reviews of time-restricted eating and alternate-day fasting. Deep understanding of pooled effect sizes, heterogeneity moderators, and risk of bias assessment.", "question_focus": "What pooled effect sizes on glycemic, lipid, anthropometric, and blood-pressure endpoints have the strongest GRADE-high certainty in metabolic-syndrome populations, and how do protocol families (TRE, ADF, 5:2, FMD) differ in effect magnitude?"}, {"perspective": "Public_Health", "name": "Dr. Marcus Haverford", "expertise": "Cardiovascular epidemiologist with expertise in NHANES cohort analysis, observational study design, and the critical appraisal of long-term mortality signals. Prominent methodologist in the debate around the 2024 AHA EPI/Lifestyle abstract on time-restricted eating and cardiovascular death.", "question_focus": "How should clinicians reconcile the short-term supervised RCT benefit with population-level observational cardiovascular mortality signals, and what methodological limitations constrain confidence in either direction?"}, {"perspective": "Regional", "name": "Dr. Fatima Al-Rashid", "expertise": "Clinical nutritionist at a Gulf-region academic medical center specializing in Ramadan-fasting physiology and metabolic outcomes in Muslim adults with diabetes and metabolic syndrome. Lead investigator on case-crossover Ramadan studies.", "question_focus": "What does the Ramadan intermittent-fasting systematic-review evidence contribute as a real-world counterweight to Western supervised trials, and how does it inform generalizability and safety in non-Western populations?"}, {"perspective": "Industry", "name": "Rachel Kim, PharmD, MPH", "expertise": "Health-economics and regulatory affairs specialist focused on the commercial intermittent-fasting app market, FTC substantiation standards, and the interaction between consumer IF tools and medical practice. Advises on market-research syntheses.", "question_focus": "How do real-world adherence and retention data from commercial IF apps compare with supervised-RCT effect sizes, and what is the regulatory landscape around consumer claims and clinical efficacy?"}, {"perspective": "Emerging_Trends", "name": "Dr. Anders Lindqvist", "expertise": "Translational researcher in chrononutrition, gut microbiome-immune signaling, and adipose tissue remodeling. Active in the IL-22/ILC3 beigeing literature and the transition from animal-model mechanism to human clinical trial.", "question_focus": "Which mechanistic pathways — circadian alignment, metabolic flexibility, IL-22/ILC3 gut-immune signaling, adipose beigeing — most plausibly explain clinical benefits, and where does the animal-to-human translation gap sit?"}]}
